entities.name: Careerspan
entities.context: V's company focused on candidate assessment and hiring intelligence

entities.name: Careerspan story
entities.context: Self-reflection artifact that candidates complete as part of the Careerspan process

entities.name: behavioral investment
entities.context: Signal that is harder to fake than cognitive output; the core valuable signal in hiring

entities.name: proof of work
entities.context: Demonstrable expenditure of time and attention as a scarce, valuable signal

entities.name: costly signaling
entities.context: Evolutionary biology principle: reliable signals must be costly enough that low-quality actors cannot mimic them

entities.name: engagement metrics
entities.context: Measurements of candidate effort that should be prioritized over semantic content analysis

entities.name: friction-completion
entities.context: Metric measuring a candidate's completion of effortful tasks despite friction

entities.name: coachability
entities.context: Trait signaled by willingness to engage in deep self-reflection

entities.name: conscientiousness
entities.context: Trait signaled by behavioral investment in the process

entities.name: deep self-reflection
entities.context: The activity of introspection that serves as a powerful hiring signal

relationships.context: Careerspan uses the story artifact to measure candidate engagement
Should prioritize engagement metrics over semantic content analysis
Friction-completion as a key metric for identifying high-intent candidates
Willingness to reflect deeply demonstrates behavioral investment
Behavioral investment serves as proof of work in a market flooded with AI-generated content
Proof of work follows the evolutionary biology principle of costly signaling
Behavioral investment serves as proxy for coachability
Behavioral investment serves as proxy for conscientiousness
